methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Keen Technologies is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $20M to date, founded by John Carmack. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms and one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ), though narrow peer matching proved constrained.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, using the $20M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $50M. A peer-median approach was not applicable; while seven robotics and manufacturing companies exist in the broader sector universe, none met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier needed to establish a robust narrow peer set (minimum three datapoints required). Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of robotics subsegments and capital intensity profiles.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $42M (low) to $57M (high), with a median of $50M. This analysis rests on a single methodology and thus carries elevated sensitivity to the stage-progression multiplier assumption. Key uncertainties include unit economics validation, capital efficiency relative to the broader robotics sector cohort, and whether Carmack's founder profile should warrant valuation uplift relative to stage norms. Additional financial metrics and technical milestone data would strengthen conviction.
